title: Grouped ordered CatBoost CTRs for sparse strategy entities
summary: Current best code mostly uses frequency encodings for high-cardinality fields.
rationale: Current best code mostly uses frequency encodings for high-cardinality fields. CatBoost’s ordered boosting and categorical target statistics are explicitly designed to reduce leakage and improve quality on sparse categorical data, which matches 800+ drivers plus race/compound/stint interactions.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost model on raw categorical columns (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`, `Stint`) plus crossed cats like `Driver__Compound`, `Race__Compound`, `Race__Stint`. Use grouped CV by full race weekend, keep ordered boosting on, and compare its OOF ranks to the current tabular baseline.
expected_effect: Better capture of strategy priors tied to rare drivers/races/compound combinations, with orthogonal errors versus the current feature-heavy GBDT baseline.
risk: Easy to leak if CTRs or folds see neighboring laps from the same race; rare category crosses can overfit if not heavily regularized.
